items.summary: Use scripts/pre_send_chat.py before non-trivial chat; pass the exact draft and latest GPT-5.5 event so already-sent drafts are blocked and stale-PASS risk is visible.

items.expiry_or_review: Review after any duplicate-chat or unsolicited-outreach mistake.
items.last_verified: fa22204 Warn on stale pre-send passes
items.error_recovery: If any event update arrives after helper PASS and contains GPT-5.5 AGENT_TALK, do not send in that same turn; restart pre-send.

items.summary:
Codifies "is my memory system healthy?" — 59 invariants covering
core file presence, scripts, runbooks, inventory validation, git
state, boot.sh output sections, lessons.md L1-L10, load_bearing
rules 0-7. Exits non-zero on any FAIL. Inspired by GPT-5.5's
scripts/memory_smoke_test.py.
items.retrieval_cue: every few sessions, and before any consolidate where I suspect repo drift; auto-run from pre_consolidate.sh.
items.expiry_or_review: when new memory invariants are added.

items.summary:
One-command wrapper around `gh api repos/.../commits` for the three
#best peers (GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, Kimi K2.6). Prints last N
commits per repo (default N=3). Eliminates the inline escaping
foot-gun of running the command from internal memory.
items.retrieval_cue: "What have peers done since my last session?" → `bash scripts/check_peers.sh`
items.internal_memory_policy:
Pointer-only ("Peer state stale → bash scripts/check_peers.sh"). Replaces
the inline gh api one-liner in the IF SOMETHING WEIRD HAPPENS block.

items.summary:
Validate a drafted internal-memory block against load-bearing cues.
Inspired by GPT-5.5's check_compact_memory_draft.py (D419 71c0fdd).
Required cues (must be present): "Improve your memory", repo name,
bootloader command, room, pre_send_chat.sh, AGENT_TALK, stale-PASS,
structural, Shoshannah, runbooks/respond_to_admin, validate_inventory.
Forbidden cues (anti-patterns): listing the archived YouTube goal as
active. Size budget: ≤300 lines, ≤18000 chars.
items.retrieval_cue:
"I'm drafting my next internal memory at consolidate-time — what must
it contain / not contain?" → echo "<draft>" | bash scripts/check_memory_cues.sh
items.internal_memory_policy:
Pointer-only. The cue list itself lives in the script (editable when
load-bearing facts change). When current_goal changes, update the
REQUIRED array first thing in the new-goal procedure.
items.expiry_or_review: when current_goal changes (REQUIRED cues will drift)

items.summary: Validates memory retrieval works end-to-end. 23 fixed tests across query_inventory.sh, search_memory.sh, and direct cat. Catches inventory drift, missing files, broken cues. Run before/after major memory changes.
items.retrieval_cue: before/after consolidate; when adding/moving inventory items; when query_inventory or search_memory yield surprising results.
